zh.lifeSummary: 當Joane Webb於1423年出生在英國肯特福利頓登時，她的父親John Alexander Webb I18歲，母親Johanna Hall18歲。她於大約1442年與John Payne在英國肯特福利頓登結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1463年4月12日之前在她的家鄉，埋葬在英國肯特福利頓登。

nameMeanings.name: Webb
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a weaver, from early Middle English webbe (Old English webba (masculine) or webbe (feminine), probably used of both male and female weavers). This word survived into Middle English long enough to give rise to the surname, but was already obsolescent as an agent noun; hence the secondary forms with the agent suffixes -er and -ster (see Webster , Webber and compare Weaver ).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish (Ashkenazic) surnames, cognates of 1, including Weber and Weberman.
History: Richard Webb, a Lowland Scot, was an admitted freeman of Boston in 1632, and in 1635 was one of the first settlers of Hartford, CT.

variants.name: Webster
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a weaver, from Middle English webbester ‘weaver’ (Old English webbestre ‘female weaver’). By the time of surname formation, the gender distinction of the -stre suffix had almost completely disappeared. Compare Webb , Webber , and Weaver .

History: The name Webster was brought to North America from England independently by several different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. One John Webster settled in Ipswich, MA, in 1635; another John Webster (died 1661), ancestor of the lexicographer Noah Webster, emigrated to Cambridge, MA, c. 1631 and later became one of the founders of the colony of CT, of which he was appointed governor in 1656. Daniel Webster (1782–1852), politician and orator, was born in Salisbury, NH, a descendant of Thomas Webster, a prominent 17th-century citizen of Ipswich, MA, whose family had settled there around 1635, while he was still a child.

variants.name: Webber
meanings: English (Devon and Somerset): occupational name for a weaver, from Middle English webber, an agent derivative of Webb .
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Weber   ‘weaver’.

nameMeanings.name: Joane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jean
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Joane Webb was born in 1423, in Frittenden, Kent, England, her father, John Alexander Webb I, was 18 and her mother, Johanna Hall, was 18. She married John Payne about 1442, in Frittenden, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died before 12 April 1463, in her hometown, and was buried in Frittenden, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joane Webb est née en 1423, à Frittenden, Kent, Angleterre, son père, John Alexander Webb I, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Johanna Hall, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé John Payne environ 1442, à Frittenden, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée avant 12 avril 1463, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterrée à Frittenden, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joane Webb 1423 in Frittenden, Kent, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Alexander Webb I 18 und ihre Mutter Johanna Hall 18. Sie hat um 1442 in Frittenden, Kent, England John Payne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb vor 12. April 1463 in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Frittenden, Kent, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Joane Webbが1423年にイギリスケントフリッテンデンで生まれた時，父John Alexander Webb Iは18歳，母Johanna Hallは18歳であった。約1442年にイギリスケントフリッテンデンでJohn Payneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1463年4月12日以前に故郷で死去し，イギリスケントフリッテンデンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joane Webb(이)가 1423년 영국 켄트 프리텐든에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Alexander Webb I(은)는 18세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Johanna Hall(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 대략 1442년 영국 켄트 프리텐든에서 John Payne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1463년 4월 12일 이전 그녀의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 켄트 프리텐든에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joane Webb родилась в 1423 г. в местности фриттенден, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени John Alexander Webb I было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Johanna Hall, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Payne около 1442 г. в местности фриттенден, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла перед 12 апрель 1463 г. в её родном городе и была похоронена в местности фриттенден, кэнт, Англия.
